# figure-out probe files — index

Domain probing fuel: angles that are easy to under-weight during understanding. Each file is **awareness, not an agenda** — load the matching one to put its angles in view, then press only what's load-bearing for the topic at hand. Most probes won't apply to any given task; that's expected, not a gap.

Detect the topic's domain from the indicators below and load the matching file. A topic can match more than one — a bug fix that restructures loads both `BUG.md` and `REFACTOR.md`. No match → general probing only, no file needed.

| Domain | Indicators | File |
|--------|------------|------|
| Coding (base) | Any code change | `CODING.md` |
| Feature | New functionality, APIs, enhancements | `FEATURE.md` |
| Bug | Defects, regressions, "broken", "not working" | `BUG.md` |
| Refactor | Restructuring, cleanup, pattern changes without behavior change | `REFACTOR.md` |

`CODING.md` holds the angles common to all code change; compose it with the specific file (`FEATURE` / `BUG` / `REFACTOR`) when one applies.

This index is figure-out's own — it is independent of `/define`'s task files, which carry Quality Gates and Defaults for a different consumer.
